title: BikeWatch
description: An interactive map visualization of BlueBikes traffic patterns in the Boston area, with time filtering and cycling isochrones.

title: Campus Mobility Map
description: A quick explorable map prototype for commuting patterns around Cambridge.
story: I made this for a GIS class and honestly didn't expect it to turn into anything. The hardest part was getting the basemap tiles to not look terrible at different zoom levels. I ended up learning way more about projections than I wanted to.

title: Housing Affordability Explorer
description: Small multiples for rent burden and income bands, with filters for neighborhood.
story: The original version had a single bar chart and it was boring. I switched to small multiples after seeing some examples in class and it made the neighborhood differences actually jump out. Getting the color scales right took forever though.

title: Real Estate Carbon Budget Dashboard
description: A simple view comparing operational vs embodied emissions under retrofit scenarios.
story: This came out of a group project where we had to pick a sustainability topic. I was the only one who wanted to do embodied carbon so I kind of ran with it. The toggle between operational and embodied views was my favorite part to build.

title: Transit Catchment Story Map
description: A narrative map showing walk sheds and transfer friction across key stations.
story: I ride the T every day and always thought the transfer at Park Street was worse than it looks on paper. This was basically me trying to prove that with data. Turns out I was right, the walk times between platforms are way longer than the schedule assumes.

title: Solar Potential Quick Look
description: A lightweight calculator to sanity-check rooftop PV capacity by building type.
story: A friend was looking at solar panels for their parents house and asked me to help with the math. I turned it into a little web tool because I figured other people might want to do the same thing. Pretty simple but people actually used it which was nice.

title: Retail Footfall Heatmap
description: Weekday vs weekend foot traffic patterns for a corridor, grouped by hour.
story: The data for this was a pain to clean, lots of missing hours and some sensors that just stopped working mid-week. But once I got it into a grid the weekday vs weekend pattern was really obvious. Weekends peak around 2pm, weekdays have that lunch rush bump.

title: Zoning Change Timeline
description: A clean timeline of rezoning events, with quick links to the underlying documents.
story: I got tired of scrolling through PDFs on the city website trying to find when specific zoning changes happened. So I scraped the meeting minutes and built a timeline. Not fancy but it saves me a lot of time when I need to look something up for a paper.

title: Flood Risk Parcel Viewer
description: Parcel-level lookup for flood zones and elevation, built for fast what-if checks.
story: For a real estate finance class we had to evaluate a site and I wanted to check flood risk quickly without going through the clunky FEMA map tool. Leaflet made the map part easy, the annoying bit was joining the parcel boundaries to the flood zone polygons.

title: Construction Cost Sensitivity Tool
description: A small sensitivity grid for hard-cost inflation, interest rates, and stabilized NOI.
story: My professor kept saying run the sensitivity in class and I realized I did not have a good way to do that quickly. This is basically spreadsheet logic but in the browser so I can tweak assumptions and see the IRR change in real time.

title: Noise Complaints Analyzer
description: A quick pass at cleaning and summarizing 311 complaint text by topic and geography.
story: I pulled this from Boston 311 open data. Most of the work was text cleaning because people describe noise complaints in wildly different ways. Loud music and neighbor playing bass at 3am are the same thing but you would never know from the raw data.

title: Street Tree Equity Audit
description: A simple comparison of canopy coverage vs heat exposure across census tracts.
story: Someone in my cohort mentioned that tree coverage correlates with income and I wanted to see if that was true in Boston. It is, pretty strongly. The visualization is just a scatter plot with a regression line but it gets the point across.

title: Data Center Load Monitor
description: Sketch of an interface to track interconnection requests and projected MW demand.
story: This is still a work in progress. I am interested in how data center growth is affecting grid planning and wanted a dashboard to track the interconnection queue. Right now it just pulls from a static CSV but I want to hook it up to live data eventually.
